issues.description: Deterministic honesty record (claim_audit_unmodified, severity=should_fix): Player claim: Player claimed file coverage_task_rip_001.json. Actual: Path is tracked in git but 'git status --porcelain' shows no change for it — the Player claimed work on a file it did not actually modify this turn. Most likely cause: the report writer swept an orchestrator-managed path (e.g. a file under .guardkit/autobuild/ or tasks/<state>/) into files_modified. Defence-in-depth for the agent_invoker-side filter; this is a warning, not a turn-rejecting fabrication..

issues.description: BDD scenario 'test_published_episodes_are_structured_so_the_relay_routes_them_to_the_deterministic_writer' is PENDING (not passing). The scenario shows: 'All traceback entries are hidden. Pass `--full-trace` to see hidden and internal frames.' This represents absent evidence for end-to-end validation of the routing contract (AC-002). The unit tests pass in isolation but the BDD oracle that confirms integration with RelayService routing has not successfully completed.
issues.requirement: AC-002: The published episode routes to DeterministicWriter via RelayService._ingest_json
issues.suggestion: Investigate why the BDD scenario is pending. Run: pytest features/re-index-pipeline/ -k test_published_episodes_are_structured --full-trace -v to see the full traceback. Ensure the scenario can execute and passes, confirming end-to-end routing behavior.

issues.description: Turn 2 report lists files_modified including src/fleet_memory/reindex/publisher.py, tests/unit/reindex/test_publisher.py, and src/fleet_memory/reindex/walker.py, but honesty verification shows 'git status --porcelain' reports no changes to these files in Turn 2. The report also states 'Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0' which contradicts the files_modified list. This suggests Turn 2 may not have performed actual work, or is incorrectly reporting prior turn's files.
issues.suggestion: Clarify what work was performed in Turn 2. If files were created in Turn 1 and are correct, the Turn 2 report should reflect files_modified: [] or only list orchestrator files (.guardkit/bdd/*.xml, coverage*.json). Do not list implementation files that weren't touched this turn.

criteria_verification.notes: Independent test verification confirms publish_episode() function exists and accepts BasePayload. Test test_episode_is_json_with_payload_type and others passed.

Unit tests verify content_format='json' and payload_type are set correctly. However, the BDD scenario that validates end-to-end routing through RelayService._ingest_json to DeterministicWriter is PENDING (not passing). Without BDD confirmation, we lack evidence the routing contract works in integration. This is a blocking absence of evidence per absence-of-failure-is-not-success.md.
Independent test test_body_round_trips_through_registry passed, confirming canonical JSON serialization with sort_keys=True and successful round-trip through get_model_for_type().
Independent tests test_episode_id_deterministic_for_natural_key and test_source_ref_preserved passed, confirming SHA-256 deterministic episode_id and source_ref provenance tracking.
Independent test test_no_network_calls_during_publish passed. Publisher uses only stdlib (hashlib, json) and broker.publish with no LLM client imports.

Independent test run confirms tests/unit/reindex/test_publisher.py contains 5 tests covering all required assertions: content_format, payload_type, body round-trip, deterministic episode_id, and no network calls. All 5/5 tests passed.
Evidence bundle confirms files pass Black and Ruff checks per project code-style.md standards.

rationale: Cannot approve due to BDD scenario pending status. While unit tests (5/5) pass independently and demonstrate the publisher contract works in isolation, the BDD oracle that validates end-to-end routing behavior (AC-002) is pending, not passing. This represents absent evidence for the critical integration requirement. Per absence-of-failure-is-not-success.md, a pending scenario is not evidence of passing behavior. Additionally, honesty verification shows Turn 2 made no git-detectable changes to implementation files, yet claims them as modified, suggesting either no work was done or incorrect reporting. Require BDD scenario resolution before approval.
